devdoc.details: This is the most critical contract in the system.      Key design decisions:      - Access is granted BY the owner TO any address (doctor, attorney, auditor, family).      - Grants can be scoped to specific record IDs (empty array = all records).      - Grants carry a tier (read-only vs write) and an optional expiry.      - Every access event is logged immutably on-chain.      - The admin can register "verified providers" (doctors, law firms) but        the owner decides who gets access — admin cannot override this.

returns.allowed: True if access is currently valid.
returns.tier: The access tier granted (only meaningful when allowed == true).

params.accessor: The address receiving access (doctor, attorney, auditor).
params.durationSeconds: How long the grant is valid. 0 = permanent.
params.recordIds: Specific record IDs to allow. Empty = all records.

params.patient: The owner whose record was accessed.

registerProvider(address).details: Registration does NOT grant them access to any patient — it only      marks them as a verified entity that patients can discover and choose.

devdoc.title: AccessController

checkAccess(address,address,uint256).notice: Check whether `accessor` is currently allowed to access `recordId`         belonging to `patient`.

grantAccess(address,uint8,uint256,uint25.notice: Grant access to any address for a configurable tier and duration.

isActiveAccessor(address,address).notice: Returns true if a grant exists and has not yet expired.         Does NOT check record-level restrictions.

logAccess(address,uint256,uint256,string.notice: Append an access event to the patient's immutable audit log.

registerProvider(address).notice: Register a healthcare provider / law firm / verified accessor.

registeredProviders(address).notice: verified providers registered by admin (optional — owner can grant to any address)

userdoc.notice: Fine-grained, time-limited, per-record access control with immutable audit log.
